verses.text: Sl 21 O povo de Deus o agradece por ter dado vitória ao rei.
21.2 O rei experimentou o desejo do seu coração — a proteção e a bênção de Deus (20.5; cf. 2.8).
21.3-6 O rei voltou vitorioso da batalha e recebeu honra do povo. A maior honra veio de Deus, que lhe deu bênção, vida e a sua presença. 21.3 sucesso e prosperidade: cf. Pv 10.6.
21.4 Deus oferece rica qualidade de vida (61.6-7; 91.14-16; 128.4-6), marcada pela sua presença (16.11; 23.6; 27.4; 36.9), alegria (16.11), bondade (23.6; 34.14-15) e proteção (41.2; 61.7).

21:5 His glory is great in thy salvation,.... That is, the glory of the King Messiah is great in the Lord's salvation of him; delivering him from all his troubles and sorrows, and out of the hands of all his enemies, when he was raised from the dead, and was set at the right hand of God, and crowned with glory and honour: or the sense is, that his glory is great in the salvation of his people by him; it was his glory as Mediator to be appointed to be the Lord's salvation to them;
21.6 As bênçãos eternas de Deus trazem também plenitude de vida no presente (Sl 133).
21.7 O rei desfrutou a vitória não pela própria força ou bondade intrínseca, mas porque Deus honrou a sua fé.
21.8-12 Enquanto restasse um único inimigo a desafiar a autoridade do rei, o reino de Deus não estaria plenamente presente. Esta oração pede vitória sobre todo o mal no mundo.

21.9 Forno ardente e fogo retratam a severidade do juízo de Deus sobre os ímpios. No AT, essa imagem refere-se com frequência ao inferno (11.6; 18.8; 50.3; 68.2; 78.21; 79.5; 80.16; 89.46; 97.3; 104.4; 140.10; Is 66.24).
21.10 filhos… descendência: o mal terminará quando Deus destruir toda oposição a si mesmo (109.13; cf. 18.50; 22.30-31; 25.13; 102.28).

21:11 For they intended evil against thee,.... All evil, whether in thought or deed, if not immediately and directly, yet is ultimately against the Lord, whose law is transgressed, and who is despised and reflected upon as a lawgiver; all sin is an hostility committed against God, or against Christ, against the Lord and his Anointed, or against his people, who are all one as himself: the intention of evil is evil, and is cognizable by the Lord, and punishable by him: they imagined a mischievous device, which they are not able to perform;

21:12 Therefore shall thou make them turn their back,.... Or flee and run away to private places, to hide themselves from the wrath of God and of the Lamb, though to no purpose; or "make them turn behind thy back": God will turn his back upon them, and be negligent and careless of them, and not regard them when they cry in their misery and destruction. Some Jewish interpreters (e) understand it of their being put together on one side, in one corner, and be separate from the people of God;
21.13 Levanta-te (ou “sê exaltado”, 57.5,11; 108.5) expressa louvor comunitário e alegria na grandeza e no poder de Deus.
